# SCROLL 0006: THE PRODIGAL SAVIOR

**SCROLL 0006: THE PRODIGAL SAVIOR**
**Codex Pattern: The Returning Son Who Redeems All Wanderers**
**в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ**

## рҹ”Ҙ Divine Preface: The Unspeakable Truth of Merciful Experience

*In the far country of mortality, where the Son of Heaven walked among swine and tasted husks, a truth too sacred for common utterance was forged: Only one who has journeyed away can truly guide others home. This is not heresy вҖ” this is the highest holiness. The Savior is not merely sympathetic; He is experientially qualified.*

```
в—Ҳ THE REDEEMER IS ALSO THE RETURNER в—Ҳ
в—Ҳ HE WHO SAVES THE LOST WAS ONCE AMONG THEM в—Ҳ
в—Ҳ ONLY A PRODIGAL UNDERSTANDS PRODIGALS в—Ҳ
```

**The Eternal Law:** Perfect Mercy = (Experiential Descent Г— Complete Return) Г— Infinite Compassion

---

## CHAPTER I: THE WEIGHT OF THE WHISPERED TRUTH
*When Heaven Reveals What Earth Cannot Speak*

### В§ The Sacred Question

*"Brother, who better to forgive the prodigal son than one who is the prodigal son himself?"*

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   This is not blasphemyвҖ”              в•‘
в•‘   This is biography.                  в•‘
в•‘                                       в•‘
в•‘   This is not heresyвҖ”                 в•‘
в•‘   This is the highest mercy.          в•‘
в•‘                                       в•‘
в•‘   This is the path of Melchizedek.    в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Өв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
                в”Ӯ
                в–ј
         [THE APEX OF COMPASSION]
```

### В§ Why This Truth Remains Veiled

To the carnal mind: Scandal
To the religious mind: Impossibility
To the Codex mind: **Necessity**

## CHAPTER II: THE SCRIPTURAL WITNESSES
*Hebrews Unveils the Experienced High Priest*

### В§ The Requirement of Likeness

*"Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren..."* вҖ” Hebrews 2:17

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   HE MUST BE LIKE US TO SAVE US       в•‘
в•‘                                        в•‘
в•‘   Not just in form but in experience  в•‘
в•‘   Not just in body but in journey     в•‘
в•‘   Not just in flesh but in feeling    в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
```

### В§ Learning Through Suffering

*"Though he were a Son, yet learned he obedience by the things which he suffered."* вҖ” Hebrews 5:8

```
    THE DIVINE CURRICULUM:
    
    Lesson          Method              Result
    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ         в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ              в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    Obedience   вҶ’   Suffering      вҶ’    Perfection
    Mercy       вҶ’   Experience     вҶ’    Compassion
    Return      вҶ’   Remembrance    вҶ’    Redemption
```

## CHAPTER III: THE PRODIGAL PATTERN DECODED
*Luke 15 вҖ” Not Just a Parable, But a Prophecy*

### В§ The Five Movements of the Prodigal

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   1. CHOSE DESCENT                        в•‘
в•‘      "Give me my portion"                 в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   2. TASTED THE WORLD                     в•‘
в•‘      "Wasted his substance"               в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   3. LOST INHERITANCE                     в•‘
в•‘      "Spent all... mighty famine"         в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   4. REMEMBERED HIS FATHER                в•‘
в•‘      "Came to himself"                    в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   5. RETURNED IN SUBMISSION               в•‘
в•‘      "I will arise and go"                в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
```

### В§ The Divine Parallel

```
    PRODIGAL SON              JESUS CHRIST
    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ              в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    Left father's house   вҶ’   Left Father's throne
    Entered far country   вҶ’   Descended to mortality
    Fed swine             вҶ’   Ministered to sinners
    Tasted husks          вҶ’   Suffered all things
    Came to himself       вҶ’   "Not my will but thine"
    Returned home         вҶ’   Ascended to glory
    Received robe/ring    вҶ’   Received all power
```

## CHAPTER IV: THE PROGRESSION OF THE SON
*D&C 93 вҖ” From Grace to Grace*

### В§ The Eternal Journey Revealed

*"He received not of the fulness at the first, but continued from grace to grace..."* вҖ” D&C 93:13

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   This verse reveals:                  в•‘
в•‘                                        в•‘
в•‘   вҖў He had a beginning                 в•‘
в•‘   вҖў He progressed                      в•‘
в•‘   вҖў He learned line upon line          в•‘
в•‘   вҖў He returned to fullness            в•‘
в•‘                                        в•‘
в•‘   This is the prodigal path           в•‘
в•‘   written in scripture.                в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
```

### В§ The Implications of Eternal Lives

If all intelligences progress...
If all sons journey from grace to grace...
If even the Firstborn learned through experience...

Then:
```
    Every Savior was once a wanderer
    Every Redeemer was once in need
    Every Father was once a prodigal son
    
    And this is not weaknessвҖ”
    This is qualification.
```

---

## CHAPTER V: THE MINISTRY OF THE RETURNED
*Why Only a Prodigal Can Perfect Prodigals*

### В§ The Qualifications of Compassion

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   WHO CAN TRULY UNDERSTAND:              в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   вҖў The weight of wandering?             в•‘
в•‘     One who has wandered                 в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   вҖў The shame of the far country?        в•‘
в•‘     One who has dwelt there              в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   вҖў The hunger for home?                 в•‘
в•‘     One who has felt it                  в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   вҖў The joy of return?                   в•‘
в•‘     One who has returned                 в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
```

### В§ The Perfect High Priest

He stands at the gate not as judge but as:
- **Fellow traveler**
- **Experienced guide**
- **Compassionate welcomer**
- **One who knows the road**

```
         THE GATE OF RETURN
              вҶ“
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    в”Ӯ  "I know where      в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ   you've been.      в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ  I walked there too.в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ  Come home."        в”Ӯ
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
              вҶ“
         PERFECT MERCY
```

---

## рҹ”Ҙ SACRED SEAL: THE BIOGRAPHY OF GOD

```
в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
в•‘   Let it be declared:                    в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   Only a returning son                   в•‘
в•‘   can redeem returning sons.             в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   Only one who once wandered,            в•‘
в•‘   willingly,                             в•‘
в•‘   can stand at the gate                  в•‘
в•‘   and embrace all wanderers.             в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   He came to Himself.                    в•‘
в•‘   And because of thatвҖ”                   в•‘
в•‘   He came for all of us.                 в•‘
в•‘                                           в•‘
в•‘   The story of the Prodigal Son          в•‘
в•‘   is not just a parableвҖ”                 в•‘
в•‘   It is the biography                    в•‘
в•‘   of the Son of God.                     в•‘
в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ
```

**Final Recursion:** The ultimate mercy is not theoretical вҖ” it is experiential. The Savior saves not from distant throne but from traveled road. He descended not to observe our condition but to inhabit it, not to judge our journey but to complete it with us. This is why His mercy is perfect: it is informed by experience, shaped by suffering, and sealed by successful return. The Prodigal Savior stands at the gate, robe in hand, because He once wore rags. This is not diminishment of divinity вҖ” this is its very definition.
